_meta.purpose: Curated FireRed gate/questline knowledge — the PRIMARY data source for the Questline Deriver (questline.py). Recognise a gate -> derive how to unlock it -> execute as nav goals. GuideSearch is the fallback when this KB is insufficient.
_meta.standing_rule: We deliberately store DIRECTIONS + named places + authoritative FLAGS — NOT disasm map NUMBERS. The disasm map_groups.json list-index does NOT equal the runtime SB1 map number: verified 2026-06-28 it gives ROUTE24=(3,42)/VERMILION=(3,25) while LIVE RAM gives Route24=(3,43) (Cerulean's north edge) and the verified city-block has Vermilion=(3,5). Directions (north/south) and flag ids ARE reliable; map numbers are learned LIVE by the world-model as she walks. The Deriver routes via the live world-model + these direction hints, and confirms each step by its success FLAG.

FLAG_GOT_SS_TICKET.note: Set when Bill gives the S.S. Ticket. While UNSET, CeruleanCity_OnTransition->BlockExits parks the Slowbro/Lass/Policeman on the south exit (data/maps/CeruleanCity/scripts.inc).

FLAG_TEMP_13.note: Visibility flag of the Cerulean cut tree object at (26,32). Secondary obstacle, not the immediate gate.

exit_gates._doc: map_key 'g,n' -> { direction|area -> gate }. A gate names the FLAG that must be SET for the exit to open, plus the in-world blocker, so the recogniser can classify a blocked exit as a STORY gate (not a nav bug) and the deriver knows what to unlock. Flag state is read LIVE — a set flag means no gate.

south.blocker: a man and his Slowbro parked across the road (script: CeruleanCity_EventScript_BlockExits)
south.human: the road south out of Cerulean is blocked right now — a guy and his Slowbro won't budge until I've run an errand up north

capabilities._doc: key (a flag/item/HM name, matching Gate.missing) -> how to OBTAIN it. obtain.prereq is another capability key (chains form the questline graph). 'from' is the map the typical encounter is on; 'dir' is the coarse direction to head FROM there (reliable); 'place_name' is the destination; 'gives_cap'/'sets_flag' is the success condition the executor confirms LIVE. No map numbers (see standing_rule).

FLAG_GOT_SS_TICKET.name: the S.S. Ticket

obtain.place_name: Bill's house, past the Nugget Bridge to the north

FLAG_GOT_SS_TICKET.human: the S.S. Ticket — a guy named Bill, in the house past the Nugget Bridge to the north, hands it over

cut.name: HM01 Cut

obtain.place_name: the S.S. Anne, docked at Vermilion's harbour (south)

cut.human: HM Cut — the seasick captain of the S.S. Anne in Vermilion gives it to you once you've boarded with the S.S. Ticket
